steps.title: Center the narrator's confusion
steps.content: The narrator recalls a pedestrian trip and explains that his path wound so confusingly that he no longer knew which direction led to the village where he planned to stop. That admission is the central idea.

steps.title: Reject added outcomes
steps.content: Choice B adds fond memories, but the passage emphasizes confusion. Choice C says the location was frequently visited, which is not stated. Choice D says he overcame the difficulty, but the excerpt does not describe a solution. The main idea is that he remembers getting lost on a trip. The answer is A.
